mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market recovery that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising industrial production indicate synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-bound rate environment, surges in residential fixed investment act as a leading indicator of credit expansion, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when RSAFS rises while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.5% triggers inflation hedging flows into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as investors rotate into real assets.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL jumps above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained aggregate demand recovery, which mechanically supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates a supply-constrained expansion that historically precedes equity drawdowns due to margin compression and rate hike expectations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million annualized units, it signals robust consumer confidence and construction activity, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST crosses above 1500 (t-test p > 0.10).
